mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls signals improving labor demand and consumer income, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value 142071K shows continued growth post-Great Recession
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during payroll expansion periods (month-over-month increase) are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, the labor market reaches full employment, signaling late-cycle dynamics and potential overheating, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as rate hike expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.3% in July 2015, approaching threshold
failure_condition: If the signal triggers but SPX forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or if the threshold does not yield significant negative average returns (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of ultra-low federal funds rate and rising consumer spending indicates accommodative monetary policy is feeding through to demand, boosting corporate cash flows and stock prices over the next month.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, 20-day forward returns are not significantly above the unconditional mean (one-sample t-test p > 0.05) over the available sample, or interaction term in regression is not significant at 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates robust supply expansion, which supports earnings growth and positive equity returns, but this effect is strongest when capacity utilization is not constraining—specifically when the economy is in an expansionary phase away from the zero lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage 107.477 indicates growth in mid-2015
failure_condition: If the signal triggers but SPX forward 20-day returns average zero or negative during expansionary periods (excluding recessions), or if Rank IC < 0.03 over full backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent consumer price inflation reduces real discount rates and signals future monetary tightening, both of which compress equity valuations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI in June 2015 at 237.8, low inflation environment
failure_condition: If the inverse relation does not hold: CPI acceleration does not precede negative returns, or cross-sectional rank IC between CPI surprise and forward returns is not negative over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening real estate and construction demand, a leading indicator of economic slowdown, which depresses corporate earnings and stock prices over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage 1174 in June 2015, relatively stable
failure_condition: If triggered instances show positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the average return is not negative and significant at 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with near-zero short-term rates indicates a 'Goldilocks' scenario of solid employment and easy monetary conditions, driving short-term positive sentiment and stock price increases within the week.
failure_condition: If the joint condition does not produce a significantly positive average 5-day return (t-test p < 0.05), or if the interaction term in a linear model is not significant.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending growth (RSAFS) signals robust demand-driven economic expansion, but this only translates to positive equity returns when the labor market is tight (low unemployment). In a slack labor market, spending growth may be unsustainable.
failure_condition: When RSAFS growth triggers and UNRATE is below 5.5%, forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive, or the same condition in a slack market does not produce weaker returns.
